behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: A pure unit test can miss real failures around raw-body capture, header parsing, and middleware wiring. The failure boundary is the HTTP handler + signature verifier + queue adapter interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Run the webhook route with a real in-process HTTP request (real raw payload bytes) and an invalid `X-Signature-256`; assert `401` and assert no queue write occurred in the persistent queue adapter/store.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: Ordering and durability between handler and persistent queue can be falsely green with mocks. Need real queue/storage semantics to catch ack-before-commit bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real queue implementation backed by local persistent storage; send a valid webhook and instrument response timing vs queue commit completion; assert queue write is durably visible before `200` is observed.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: This is async decoupling across handler, queue, and worker modules. Mocking worker completion can hide accidental `await`/blocking behavior in wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Start service with real queue and a downstream consumer that sleeps (e.g., 10s). Send valid webhook and assert HTTP response returns quickly while consumer is still running; later verify event is processed asynchronously.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This is a real-time concurrency SLO under load. In-process mocks can hide socket/backlog/event-loop contention and scheduler effects; requires real HTTP server behavior and wall-clock measurement.
behaviors.test_description: Run the service process with real HTTP listener and queue/worker, pre-load slow concurrent downstream work, then send webhook requests over real HTTP and assert each response completes under 3s wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness typically depends on persistence constraints (unique key/transactional check) across modules; mocks can miss duplicate-write races or incorrect repository usage.
behaviors.test_description: Using real persistent queue storage, submit the same event ID twice (including a concurrent variant); assert first write succeeds and second is ignored/rejected, with only one stored queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Need real interaction between handler and actual queue-full signaling path. Pure mocks may always return expected error and miss mapping/translation bugs to HTTP status/headers.
behaviors.test_description: Configure real queue capacity to a small limit, fill it, then send another valid webhook; assert HTTP `503` and presence/value of `Retry-After`, and assert no enqueue occurred.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates real persistence-backed queue behavior, not just handler logic. A unit test of counters can miss storage-level enforcement bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue with explicit capacity N in local storage; enqueue N events successfully, attempt N+1, assert rejection/error and unchanged persisted queue size.
